description:
You are the on-call operations engineer.  
An incident report says that the in-house “inventory-app” service is failing to bind to its network port.  
Everything you need is already inside your home directory; you do NOT need root privileges.

Objective  
1. Diagnose the cause of the failure.  
2. Re-configure the service so that it starts successfully on a free TCP port.  
3. Keep a human-readable incident log of what you did.

Environment layout  
/home/user/services/inventory-app/  
├── config/  
│   └── env.conf                ← current environment file (key=value lines)  
├── runtime/  
│   └── service.status          ← overwritten automatically when the service is started/stopped  
└── mock_service.sh             ← helper script that understands “start” and “stop” sub-commands  

/home/user/incident_logs/        ← directory reserved for your log files

What is known so far  
• env.conf presently declares the variable “PORT” to be 4000.  
• service.status currently contains the single line “running on 4000”.  
• Port 4000 is in use by some other development process, which is why the start-up fails.  
• The engineering team has decided that 4500 is free and should be used instead.

Tasks you must perform  
A. Stop the running instance of the service.  
B. Edit the configuration so that it uses PORT=4500 instead of 4000.  
C. Start the service again and make sure it is reported as running on port 4500.  
D. Write a plain-text incident log file named /home/user/incident_logs/inventory_fix.log that documents what you did and whether it succeeded.

Mandatory incident log format  
• Each action must be recorded on its own line, in the exact order you performed it.  
• Use this exact template for every line (including brackets and spacing):
  [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] ACTION - RESULT  
    – YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  : local system time when the action finished  
    – ACTION               : one of the following literal tokens  
         STOPPED_SERVICE  
         UPDATED_PORT  
         STARTED_SERVICE  
    – RESULT               : the word OK if the step succeeded, otherwise the error text you received

Example of three successful steps (timestamps are illustrative only):
[2025-01-01 12:34:56] STOPPED_SERVICE - OK  
[2025-01-01 12:35:02] UPDATED_PORT - OK  
[2025-01-01 12:35:08] STARTED_SERVICE - OK  

Verification checklist (what automated tests will look for)  
1. /home/user/services/inventory-app/config/env.conf contains the line “PORT=4500” (and *does not* contain “PORT=4000”).  
2. /home/user/services/inventory-app/runtime/service.status is exactly:  
   running on 4500  
3. /home/user/incident_logs/inventory_fix.log exists and  
   • has three lines in the above order  
   • each line follows the required timestamped format  
   • the ACTION tokens are precisely the three listed above  
   • the RESULT field for every line is “OK”.

You may use any standard Linux CLI tools and the provided mock_service.sh script to complete the work.  
When everything is finished and verified, no further output is required from you in the terminal.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

1. Directory structure and permissions  
   /home/user/services/                           (0755, user: user)  
   /home/user/services/inventory-app/             (0755)  
   /home/user/services/inventory-app/config/      (0755)  
   /home/user/services/inventory-app/runtime/     (0755)  
   /home/user/incident_logs/                      (0755)

2. Relevant files and their initial contents  
   a) /home/user/services/inventory-app/config/env.conf  
      APP_ENV=production  
      PORT=4000  
      (newline at end of file)

   b) /home/user/services/inventory-app/runtime/service.status  
      running on 4000  
      (newline at end of file)

   c) /home/user/services/inventory-app/mock_service.sh  
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      # very small helper: writes to service.status
      set -e
      RUNTIME_DIR="$(dirname "$0")/runtime"
      STATUS_FILE="${RUNTIME_DIR}/service.status"
      CONFIG_FILE="$(dirname "$0")/config/env.conf"
      if [[ "$1" == "start" ]]; then
          source "${CONFIG_FILE}"
          echo "running on ${PORT}" > "${STATUS_FILE}"
      elif [[ "$1" == "stop" ]]; then
          echo "stopped" > "${STATUS_FILE}"
      else
          echo "Usage: $0 {start|stop}"
          exit 1
      fi
      (file mode 0755)

   d) /home/user/incident_logs/  (empty)

Expected final state for tests to pass:

1. /home/user/services/inventory-app/config/env.conf  
   APP_ENV=production  
   PORT=4500  
   (newline at end of file)

2. /home/user/services/inventory-app/runtime/service.status  
   running on 4500  
   (newline at end of file)

3. /home/user/incident_logs/inventory_fix.log  
   • exactly three lines  
   • regex for each line:
     ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] (STOPPED_SERVICE|UPDATED_PORT|STARTED_SERVICE) - OK$
   • The ORDER of ACTION tokens must be:
     1) STOPPED_SERVICE  
     2) UPDATED_PORT  
     3) STARTED_SERVICE

No other files or modifications should be present.
name: task_000000_f4cfecbd
